title: `buildah rm --all` cannot be executed via ansible command
body:
ansible command `command: buildah rm --all` or `command: buildah rm -a` cannot be executed.

Tested similar command like `buildah images -q` can be executed in ansible.

```
fatal: []: FAILED! => {"changed": true, "cmd": ["buildah", "rm", "-a"], "delta": "0:00:00.018499", "end": "2018-01-18 01:26:41.853608", "msg": "non-zero return code", "rc": 1, "start": "2018-01-18 01:26:41.835109", "stderr": "flag provided but not defined: -a", "stderr_lines": ["flag provided but not defined: -a"], "stdout": "Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -a\n\nNAME:\n   buildah rm - Remove one or more working containers\n\nUSAGE:\n   buildah rm CONTAINER-NAME-OR-ID [...]\n\nDESCRIPTION:\n   Removes one or more working containers, unmounting them if necessary", "stdout_lines": ["Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -a", "", "NAME:", "   buildah rm - Remove one or more working containers", "", "USAGE:", "   buildah rm CONTAINER-NAME-OR-ID [...]", "", "DESCRIPTION:", "   Removes one or more working containers, unmounting them if necessary"]}
```

```
fatal: []: FAILED! => {
    "changed": true, 
    "cmd": [
        "buildah", 
        "rm", 
        "--all"
    ], 
    "delta": "0:00:00.017288", 
    "end": "2018-01-18 01:28:20.429446", 
    "invocation": {
        "module_args": {
            "_raw_params": "buildah rm --all", 
            "_uses_shell": false, 
            "chdir": null, 
            "creates": null, 
            "executable": null, 
            "removes": null, 
            "stdin": null, 
            "warn": true
        }
    }, 
    "msg": "non-zero return code", 
    "rc": 1, 
    "start": "2018-01-18 01:28:20.412158", 
    "stderr": "flag provided but not defined: -all", 
    "stderr_lines": [
        "flag provided but not defined: -all"
    ], 
    "stdout": "Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -all\n\nNAME:\n   buildah rm - Remove one or more working containers\n\nUSAGE:\n   buildah rm CONTAINER-NAME-OR-ID [...]\n\nDESCRIPTION:\n   Removes one or more working containers, unmounting them if necessary", 
    "stdout_lines": [
        "Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -all", 
        "", 
        "NAME:", 
        "   buildah rm - Remove one or more working containers", 
        "", 
        "USAGE:", 
        "   buildah rm CONTAINER-NAME-OR-ID [...]", 
        "", 
        "DESCRIPTION:", 
        "   Removes one or more working containers, unmounting them if necessary"
    ]
}
```

comments.body: the buildah version is not latest, test it works on the master.

thanks @gouyang for checking that out.
BTW, is there a way to get these ansible scripts?

@gouyang thanks for the diagnosis!  That's correct, that rm/rmi -all functionality is only on GitHub master at the moment.  But it is winding it's way through the Fedora kit process and should be in the Buildah 0.11 kit.
buildah-0.11 was released this week.  Needs one more karma to get out of updates-testing.  I have also built it for the next RHEL release.  I  have asked for it to be built for Centos and Ubuntu.
@ripcurld0 The scripts can be getting here: https://upstreamfirst.fedorainfracloud.org/buildah
